title: Ill Will

jokes.text: Move over, Kitty Kelly. Cedric has arrived.
jokes.setup: Cedric announces he's written memoirs
jokes.punchline: Compares himself to famous celebrity biographer Kitty Kelly
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Cedric's pretensions, solid reference humor

jokes.text: Through the Keyhole: Memoirs of an Embittered Butler
jokes.setup: Cedric announces his tell-all book about the wealthy family
jokes.punchline: The title plays on 'through the keyhole' (spying/gossip) and positions him as 'embittered'
jokes.explanation: Strong wordplay that establishes the episode's central conflict while characterizing Cedric as vindictive but clever

jokes.text: Check this out. Is it Cedric, or is it Patrick Swayze?
jokes.setup: Cedric admires his author photo
jokes.punchline: Compares himself to the famously handsome Patrick Swayze
jokes.explanation: Basic vanity joke relying on pop culture reference without much twist

jokes.text: Oh, get real.
jokes.setup: Family member expresses moral outrage about the exposé
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's dismissive two-word response deflates their righteousness
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and character voice - Geoffrey's dry dismissal of their fake outrage

jokes.text: Allow me to see you out, Aunt Blabby.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey offers to escort Cedric out
jokes.punchline: Calls him 'Aunt Blabby' - feminizing insult about his gossipy nature
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that's both insulting and character-appropriate for Geoffrey's wit

jokes.text: Aunt Viv, I need a nighttime sniffling, sneezing... fallen and I can't get up medicine.
jokes.setup: Will tries to convince Aunt Viv he's sick
jokes.punchline: Mixes medicine commercial with Life Alert commercial catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Creative mashup of two famous TV commercial catchphrases that perfectly captures Will's dramatic fake illness

jokes.text: I'm all better. It's a miracle. I got no more fever I got no more fever
jokes.setup: Uncle Philip doesn't believe Will is sick, then Will suddenly 'recovers'
jokes.punchline: Will instantly claims miracle recovery with gospel-style singing
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy with Will's signature over-the-top performance style

jokes.text: Aunt Viv? -Yes? -Catch me.
jokes.setup: Will claims he's miraculously recovered
jokes.punchline: Immediately faints, proving he really is sick
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection - sets up his recovery then immediately undercuts it with the faint

jokes.text: Why do you think they wear masks? So they can't be identified.
jokes.setup: Will explains why he's afraid of surgery
jokes.punchline: Suggests doctors wear masks to hide their identity like criminals
jokes.explanation: Dark but clever observation that plays on surgical anxiety with criminal imagery

jokes.text: And why do you think they knock you out? So you don't see them drinking margaritas over your open stomach.
jokes.setup: Will continues explaining his surgery fears
jokes.punchline: Absurd image of doctors partying during surgery
jokes.explanation: Escalates the paranoia with an absurdist image that's both dark and funny

jokes.text: Hey, thanks a lot, Hil, that's real nice. Well, sometimes a girl just has to treat herself.
jokes.setup: Hillary arrives with what appears to be a gift for Will
jokes.punchline: Hillary reveals she bought it for herself, not Will
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hillary characterization - her self-absorption is the entire joke

jokes.text: Don't answer that. It'll be in Geoffrey's memoirs and Urkel will play you in the TV movie.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey asks where Will keeps his pajamas
jokes.punchline: Hillary warns the intimate detail will end up in Geoffrey's tell-all, with Urkel casting
jokes.explanation: Meta humor about TV movies with perfect Urkel reference for the era

jokes.text: I, Will Smith, being of sound mind, do hereby bequeath....
jokes.setup: Carlton presenting Will with a document to sign
jokes.punchline: It's a will/testament, implying Will might die
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor, very Carlton to be practically morbid

jokes.text: Do you want just anyone... to get their hands on that $323 and 15 cents... you have hidden under your LL Cool J hat?
jokes.setup: Carlton tries to get Will to sign a will
jokes.punchline: Reveals Will's pathetically small hidden stash in a very specific location
jokes.explanation: Good character detail - the specificity of the amount and hiding place makes it funnier

jokes.text: Two bottles of beer on the wall... One million bottles of beer on the wall
jokes.setup: Will tries to delay leaving by starting the beer song
jokes.punchline: When told visiting hours are over, he jumps to 'one million bottles' to extend the stalling
jokes.explanation: Good escalation joke showing Will's desperate attempt to avoid being left alone

jokes.text: Zowie. It's showtime at the Apollo.
jokes.setup: Will's roommate Max is getting a sponge bath
jokes.punchline: Will compares the scene to the famous Apollo Theater
jokes.explanation: Cultural reference that fits Will's perspective but isn't particularly clever

jokes.text: Look like somebody already ate this.
jokes.setup: Will is served hospital dinner
jokes.punchline: Suggests the food looks pre-digested
jokes.explanation: Standard hospital food joke without much originality

jokes.text: Running around like this could be fatal. So, you'll die and I'll get another nurse.
jokes.setup: Nurse warns Max that running around could be fatal
jokes.punchline: Max matter-of-factly says she'll just get replaced
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that establishes Max's character - cavalier attitude toward death with practical outlook

jokes.text: Beautiful. I never forget a face.
jokes.setup: Max gets the nurse to bend over to pick up his 'lucky hat'
jokes.punchline: Max was checking out her body, not her face
jokes.explanation: Classic dirty old man joke that fits Max's established character

jokes.text: Man, when I get old I hope I get as much action as you. Heck, next week I hope I get as much action as you.
jokes.setup: Will admires Max's success with the nurse
jokes.punchline: Escalates from 'when I get old' to 'next week' showing his current lack of action
jokes.explanation: Good escalation structure with self-deprecating punchline

jokes.text: Man, you must be tripping. I was 15 when I first.... You mean surgery. Yes. Relax.
jokes.setup: Max asks if Will is a 'first timer' and Will misunderstands
jokes.punchline: Will thinks Max is talking about sex, not surgery
jokes.explanation: Classic misdirection that works well for Will's character and age

jokes.text: I played there 40 years ago. Bad theater, lousy audience.
jokes.setup: Nurse says Max should be in therapy
jokes.punchline: Max interprets it as theater, dismissing his past performance venue
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that reveals Max's entertainment background while avoiding medical discussion

jokes.text: Where are my glasses? Oh, hell.
jokes.setup: Doctor claims to be qualified
jokes.punchline: Immediately can't find his glasses
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective contradiction of the doctor's competence claim

jokes.text: Well, so much for golf tomorrow.
jokes.setup: Doctor examines Will's throat
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions having to cancel golf, implying Will's case is serious
jokes.explanation: Darkly casual comment that suggests something is wrong while prioritizing leisure

jokes.text: God... please don't let me leave here with breasts.
jokes.setup: Will is terrified of the incompetent doctor
jokes.punchline: His prayer reveals he's worried about random surgical mistakes
jokes.explanation: Absurdist prayer that perfectly captures surgical anxiety with an unexpected specific fear

jokes.text: Yo, man, what'd you call me? Kreplach. It's a dumpling. Hey, have a nosh.
jokes.setup: Max offers Will kreplach
jokes.punchline: Will thinks it's an insult, Max explains it's just food
jokes.explanation: Cultural clash humor that works for both characters' backgrounds

jokes.text: Rap tape? Sounded like an auction.
jokes.setup: Will asking Max about his rap tape
jokes.punchline: Max's old-school perspective on rap music
jokes.explanation: Good generation gap humor, accurate to how older people heard early rap

jokes.text: Please, don't make me cry. I can't spare the fluid.
jokes.setup: Will compliments Max emotionally
jokes.punchline: Max deflects with dark humor about his medical condition
jokes.explanation: Perfect Max line - touching moment undercut with medical dark humor and perfect economy

jokes.text: Man, I had forgotten how big Jimmie Walker's lips was.
jokes.setup: Will and Max are watching TV
jokes.punchline: Will makes an observation about Jimmie Walker's physical features
jokes.explanation: Basic observational humor without much craft or originality

jokes.text: We just brought little Billy to the hospital for a field trip. And now he's....
jokes.setup: Will is watching a dramatic TV show
jokes.punchline: The absurd premise that a field trip to a hospital resulted in a child's death
jokes.explanation: Absurdist parody of melodramatic TV shows with a dark premise

jokes.text: Cause we in here bugging out, Nurse Petty.
jokes.setup: Nurse asks why Will isn't sleeping
jokes.punchline: Will's street slang response calling her 'Nurse Petty' (petty = small/trivial)
jokes.explanation: Good character voice with mild wordplay on the nurse's behavior

jokes.text: Yo man, it's called Death Certificate, man.
jokes.setup: Will dreams about Dr. Dre as his surgeon
jokes.punchline: The album name becomes ominously appropriate for surgery
jokes.explanation: Dream sequence that connects hip-hop culture to Will's surgery fears

jokes.text: No, but I play one on TV. Yeah, you that dude from the soap opera. Hey, didn't you just die in a car crash?
jokes.setup: Will thinks the person is a real doctor
jokes.punchline: It's a soap opera actor who recently 'died' on the show
jokes.explanation: Meta humor about soap opera tropes and the famous 'I'm not a doctor but I play one on TV' line

jokes.text: Right now I'm hanging precariously on the edge of a cliff... and I intend to stay there until they put a window in my dressing room.
jokes.setup: Actor explains his current soap opera storyline
jokes.punchline: He's holding up the plot resolution for better dressing room accommodations
jokes.explanation: Creative meta joke about actor contract disputes affecting soap opera storylines

jokes.text: Tonsils don't get ratings, young man.
jokes.setup: Will says he's just getting tonsils out
jokes.punchline: Actor explains simple surgery won't make good TV
jokes.explanation: Good meta humor about TV medical drama priorities over actual medical needs

jokes.text: Yo, man, you better be using that scalpel to cut some lemon wedges for Isaac.
jokes.setup: Doctor prepares for surgery
jokes.punchline: Will references Love Boat's Isaac the bartender, suggesting the scalpel should be used for drinks
jokes.explanation: Pop culture reference that connects to the nautical theme while showing Will's fear

jokes.text: Hey, man, I don't care if you're playing shuffleboard with Nipsey Russell. I ain't getting cut by no doctor with knee socks.
jokes.setup: Love Boat actor mentions meeting Adrienne Barbeau
jokes.punchline: Will escalates with another cruise ship reference and refuses surgery from someone in costume
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with period-appropriate TV references and Will's street logic

jokes.text: I've pretended to do this operation a thousand times. Look, this time they've given me a real scalpel. Cool.
jokes.setup: Actor tries to reassure Will about the surgery
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's only pretended before and is excited about real tools
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor - the 'reassurance' makes everything worse

jokes.text: Man, I'm getting out of here before Dr. Hannibal Lecter shows up.
jokes.setup: Will is fleeing the hospital after the nightmare scenarios
jokes.punchline: References the famous cannibal doctor from Silence of the Lambs
jokes.explanation: Timely pop culture reference that escalates Will's medical fears

jokes.text: Well, Nurse, I'd run a KGB and an NAACP. If he still doesn't respond give him an upper GI Joe.
jokes.setup: Nurse asks for medical advice about non-responsive patient
jokes.punchline: Doctor gives nonsensical orders mixing medical terms with organizations and toys
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay escalating from KGB/NAACP to GI Joe - shows complete medical incompetence through absurdist humor

jokes.text: I've got to get my real estate license.
jokes.setup: Doctor gives completely nonsensical medical orders
jokes.punchline: Nurse's deadpan reaction suggests she needs a career change
jokes.explanation: Perfect reaction beat with excellent economy - suggests this incompetence is the last straw

jokes.text: Dad, let's cut the crapola and get to what's really on our minds. Yeah. I'm talking about Geoffrey's book.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil tries to have a family bonding moment about Will's surgery
jokes.punchline: Carlton immediately redirects to their real concern - Geoffrey's tell-all
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing Carlton's priorities and the family's self-absorption

jokes.text: Geoffrey knows everything about us. If Mom and Dad find out what really goes on around here, they're going to kill us.
jokes.setup: Family discusses Geoffrey's tell-all book
jokes.punchline: Hillary's dramatic escalation to murder over embarrassing revelations
jokes.explanation: Standard escalation joke that fits Hillary's dramatic personality

jokes.text: Carlton, we've got to find those memoirs. I'll just die if my friends find out why Lou in Better Shoes... gives me that 10% discount.
jokes.setup: Ashley and Carlton are searching for Geoffrey's memoirs
jokes.punchline: Ashley reveals an embarrassing secret about getting shoe store discounts
jokes.explanation: Character-specific embarrassment that adds to the family's desperation

jokes.text: I'll just die if my friends find out why Lou in Better Shoes gives me that 10% discount.
jokes.setup: Carlton worried about secrets being revealed
jokes.punchline: Implies something inappropriate for a shoe discount
jokes.explanation: Good character moment that suggests Carlton's hidden side

jokes.text: Yeah, what if my friends find out I sleep with a hand puppet?
jokes.setup: Ashley reveals her embarrassing secret
jokes.punchline: Carlton reveals his own childish secret about sleeping with a puppet
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton character detail - reveals his immaturity despite his pretensions

jokes.text: Do you really want Hillary to find out she was conceived during a Sly Stone concert?
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil threatens Vivian about the memoir revelations
jokes.punchline: Reveals embarrassing detail about Hillary's conception
jokes.explanation: Good character revelation that explains Hillary while creating family awkwardness

jokes.text: Do you really want Hilary to find out she was conceived during a Sly Stone concert?
jokes.setup: Philip threatening Vivian about family secrets
jokes.punchline: Revealing embarrassing details about Hilary's conception
jokes.explanation: Good family secret that's specific and embarrassing while being believable for the characters' past

jokes.text: Well, it never would have happened if he hadn't been five hours late.
jokes.setup: Phil reveals embarrassing conception details
jokes.punchline: Vivian's defensive response reveals more embarrassing details
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the embarrassing revelation with marital dynamic

jokes.text: What are y'all doing here? And I hope them concert tickets wasn't front row.
jokes.setup: Will catches the family in Geoffrey's room and overhears the conception story
jokes.punchline: Will makes a crack about the concert seating during conception
jokes.explanation: Good callback that adds Will's street perspective to the family embarrassment

jokes.text: Is that why you always play Hot Fun in the Summertime on my birthday?
jokes.setup: Hillary learns about her conception at a Sly Stone concert
jokes.punchline: Realizes this explains a family tradition
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that connects the revelation to established family behavior

jokes.text: Is that why you always play Hot Fun in the Summertime on my birthday?
jokes.setup: Learning about her conception at the concert
jokes.punchline: Connects it to a family tradition she never understood
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that makes a family tradition suddenly make sense

jokes.text: Probably at a white sale.
jokes.setup: Ashley asks where she was conceived
jokes.punchline: Carlton suggests the boring, practical Carlton-like location
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton characterization - suggests the most mundane, practical conception scenario

jokes.text: I mean, if I wanted to get cut I'd let Stevie Wonder do my fade.
jokes.setup: Will explains his fear of surgery
jokes.punchline: Compares surgery to letting a blind person cut his hair
jokes.explanation: Excellent Will line combining street culture (fade haircut) with clever analogy about trusting blind people with sharp objects

jokes.text: Abdullah, here's $100. If he tries to run, sit on him.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil says he trusts Will not to escape
jokes.punchline: Immediately bribes the orderly to physically restrain Will
jokes.explanation: Good contradiction between Phil's stated trust and actual precautions

jokes.text: No, I'm afraid Max has gone to a better place. Yeah, he went. Just like that.
jokes.setup: Will asks where Max is
jokes.punchline: Nurse uses euphemism that makes Will think Max died
jokes.explanation: Classic misdirection using funeral euphemisms that will be subverted later

jokes.text: Like I swallowed a weed whacker.
jokes.setup: Family asks how Will feels after surgery
jokes.punchline: Will describes his sore throat with a vivid mechanical comparison
jokes.explanation: Good Will metaphor that vividly describes post-surgical throat pain

jokes.text: I believe he referred to it as 'Sominex in a dust jacket.'
jokes.setup: Will asks what Geoffrey's literary agent said about his memoirs
jokes.punchline: Agent called it a sleeping pill in book form
jokes.explanation: Clever metaphor for boring writing that resolves the memoir subplot with Geoffrey's dignity intact

jokes.text: Max. I thought you died. In Pittsburgh I died. It wasn't my fault. I was opening for a seal.
jokes.setup: Will thought Max had died
jokes.punchline: Max explains he 'died' performing in Pittsburgh, with an absurd opening act detail
jokes.explanation: Perfect Max line - resolves the death misdirection while revealing his entertainment background with absurd detail about opening for a seal

jokes.text: Wait. The nurse said you went on to a better place. I did. Cedars-Sinai Hospital. They've got cable.
jokes.setup: Will mentions the nurse's euphemism about Max going to a better place
jokes.punchline: Max reveals the 'better place' is just a hospital with cable TV
jokes.explanation: Perfect resolution of the death misdirection with Max's practical priorities

jokes.text: I did. Cedars-Sinai Hospital. They've got cable.
jokes.setup: Will saying Max went to a better place
jokes.punchline: Better hospital with better amenities
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection joke that subverts death expectations

jokes.text: You know, maybe we could hang out sometime. Maybe. If you were a woman and I had a prostate.
jokes.setup: Will suggests they could hang out
jokes.punchline: Max's response combines his medical issues with his romantic priorities
jokes.explanation: Perfect Max character moment combining his medical condition with his romantic priorities

jokes.text: Kreplach. It's a dumpling. I got kugel here. Tsimmes, latkes. Jewish....
jokes.setup: Max is offering food to the nurse
jokes.punchline: Lists traditional Jewish foods, continuing his cultural character
jokes.explanation: Callback to Max's cultural food offerings, maintaining character consistency

jokes.text: What you got to go through for a lousy $100,000 a show.
jokes.setup: Will Smith breaks character at the end of the episode
jokes.punchline: Comments on his salary as if the hospital experience was work-related
jokes.explanation: Meta fourth-wall break typical of Fresh Prince's style, though feels somewhat disconnected from the episode

tmdb_overview: The family fears a steamy exposé when Geoffrey begins his memoirs, while Will fears the scalpel when he enters the hospital, kicking and screaming, for a tonsillectomy.
